result:
Both bugs are fixed and the full suite passes (`6 passed`). Summary of what was wrong in `lrucache/cache.py`:

1. **`get` didn't refresh recency** — despite the docstring saying reads count as a "use", `get` never moved the accessed key to the end of the `OrderedDict`. A just-read key stayed at the LRU end and got wrongly evicted on the next `put`. Fixed by adding `move_to_end` on successful reads.

2. **`put` evicted on in-place updates** — the eviction check ran unconditionally, so updating an existing key while at capacity evicted an unrelated entry. Fixed by evicting only when the key is new (`key not in self._data`).

I wrote `SOLUTION.md` documenting both root causes, and did not touch anything under `tests/`.
